verdict_reasoning: HSPA5/GRP78/BiP is canonically an ER-lumenal chaperone with no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. However, extensive literature documents that under ER stress (oncogenic transformation, hypoxia, nutrient deprivation, viral infection), HSPA5 translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. On the cell surface it acts as a receptor/co-receptor, activates PI3K/AKT signaling, promotes tumor survival and metastasis, and is targeted by cell-surface-directed monoclonal antibodies (e.g. MAb159) that bind native surface GRP78 on cancer cells but not normal fibroblasts. This is a well-documented cancer/ER-stress-induced ecto-chaperone display, fitting cell_state_induced contextual biology.
